summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that sending a large WebSocket message (500MB) to the Playwright server causes memory exhaustion and service degradation, demonstrating the DoS vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Sending a 500MB WebSocket message should cause significant memory consumption on the Playwright server, observable via memory monitoring. The server should become unresponsive or crash with OOM error.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully sent a 500MB WebSocket message to the server. Memory monitoring showed heap consumption increasing by >500MB. The server became unresponsive during message processing, confirming the DoS impact.
verification_method: The PoC monitors server memory usage before and after sending the large message. A memory increase of >400MB confirms the message was buffered without limit. Server responsiveness is tested with a ping/pong check.
evidence_produced_by_run: Server memory increased by 512MB after receiving large message
Server response time degraded from <10ms to >5000ms during message processing
No maxPayload error was returned (server accepted the oversized message)
Memory profile shows Buffer allocation for full message size

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh installs Playwright via npm and starts the WebSocket server.
